quotes.text: According to the Sages, this convocation occurred on the morrow of the Day of Atonement, when Moses descended Mount Sinai bearing the tablets, having obtained divine pardon for Israel.
quotes.speaker: Nahum Sarna
quotes.attr: Nahum Sarna, The JPS Torah Commentary: Exodus, Philadelphia, 1989, 222

notes: 2-3. The injunction is practically a verbatim repetition of 31:15, with an addition. The manner in which the prohibition against kindling fire on the Sabbath is worded led the rabbis of the Talmud to understand that fire may not be kindled on the Sabbath itself; however, fire lit before the Sabbath and not refueled on the Sabbath is permitted. The Jewish sectarians known as Karaites rejected this interpretation and
offering (teruwmah) an offering to God from (ruwm) to rise, rise up, be high, be lofty, be exalted - our offerings to the Lord exalt us.
willing (bydn nadiyb noble, generous) heart This is the foundation of a true offering.
badger (tachash) perhaps the badger or dugong (a river mammal like a walrus), dolphin, or sheep or a now extinct animal

quotes.text: The people-men and women alike-respond to Moses' call with unstinting generosity, freely contributing their most precious possessions as well as their skilled services.
quotes.speaker: Nahum Sarna
quotes.attr: Nahum Sarna, The JPS Torah Commentary: Exodus, Philadelphia, 1989, 223

quotes.text: The people are informed of God's designation of Bezalel and Oholiab as the chief artisans and supervisors of the entire project, as recorded in 31:1-11. Berakhot 55a remarks that this public announcement by Moses is to teach that one must not appoint a communal leader without first consulting the people.
quotes.speaker: Nahum Sarna
quotes.attr: Nahum Sarna, The JPS Torah Commentary: Exodus, Philadelphia, 1989, 224

notes: Just as the spirit of God moves about the waters of creation, it also moves through the workman Bezaleel: "the human artist is a craftsman who is filled with the 'spirit of God' (Ex. 35:31), the same rûah 'elohîm mentioned in Genesis 1:2 as moving over the waters of the primordial world of creation." Note that the spirit of God is also present at Nephi's construction of his work of curious workmanship (1 Nephi 17:52). (Boats, Beginnings, and Repetitions Alan Goff, FARMS Journal of Book of Mormon Studies, 1:79.) The tabernacle, like other divinely directed work projects, is done under the Lord's direction.
They are endowed with the spiritual gift of teaching others (see D&C 46:12).
